ai_overview: Yesterday's activity centered on platform security, UI component expansion, and critical hotfixes for the Cosmo Spins and Iceland launches. A key technical blocker has emerged where a race condition bug is currently obstructing desktop performance metrics due to geo-redirect issues, which requires your immediate attention. You should also follow up on the Cloudflare Bot Management and Learning Hub projects, as both have successfully transitioned into QA and are nearing completion. Cross-team dependencies are evident in the mobile space, with Gabriel Cornoiu establishing developer accounts while the team builds out New Zealand-specific widgets and social navigation tools. Finally, keep an eye on the DMCA and copyright audits being conducted by Dan Smullen and Colin Brannigan to ensure our brand protection playbooks are effectively integrated into the product workflow.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Vic was signed off on medical leave on 21 April for approximately two weeks following a doctor's appointment. Viktor's email credited her with progressing Cloudflare Bot Management into QA, but this work predates her absence. She has notified HR and Bob. No Jira or Slack activity on 21 April.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Finalized the design for the Genesis Publish Screen (GDCU-8476, now Done in Jira) and shifted focus toward the Tinder-style offer matching UX task (GDCU-8546, In Product). This interactive matching widget is her current primary priority as she transitions from the publishing workflow into more gamified user experiences. Supplementary: In #gdc-product-led-growth on 20 Apr she updated the background colour variable for the cool tone scheme to #F3F4F6 and flagged an aspect ratio mismatch between the design and production for slot game tiles.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Most recent Slack activity was on 20 Apr in #gdc-product-led-growth, where he shared a prototype for the Jump Links / Anchor Menu component (GDCU-8799) and provided feedback on the 'Jump to' label appearing disconnected from its anchor context.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Slack and Jira show otherwise. In #gdc-pod on 21 Apr she confirmed that the Cosmo Spins banner length had been fixed in line with other banners on the page. Jira shows GDCU-8893 (Gambling.com Design System) is In Progress and was updated on 21 Apr. She is leading the integration of the Claude Design tool into the Gambling.com design system, having created the project in Claude Design already and begun the design system token refactor.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Focused on developing an Instagram Headers Widget for the New Zealand market and moved the Image Carousel Shortcode into the product phase. Also managed a backlog of UI enhancements including site-wide link colour updates (GDCU-8845) and new shortcodes for brand lists and game grids. Current priorities centred on conversion rate optimisation and expanding the site's component library. Most recently posted in #gdc-core on 17 Apr with the full DC7 release notes and DC8 high-priority planned work.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Completed critical hotfixes for the Cosmo Spins sign-in flow and the Iceland launch while finalising new preset table style options. Prioritising refinements for the Global Strategy Hub and overseeing Learning Hub feedback implementation now in QA. Also documenting AI-driven time savings and researching methods to block restricted VPN users from free-to-play games (GDCU-8899). Supplementary: Active on 21 Apr in #6amigos (requested roadmap docs from Mike for upcoming cycles), #core-product (posted product sync agenda for 22 Apr), and #gdc-product-design-pod (shared sync agenda link).

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Focused on developing the Free to Play App POC v2 (GDCU-8871, In Progress), a high-priority task. Also transitioned Learning Hub feedback updates (GDCU-8733, In QA) and the redesigned sign-in and registration modals (GDCU-8803, In QA) into final QA testing. Current efforts centre on advancing the new app prototype while overseeing completion of these global UI enhancements. All three tickets confirmed active in Jira on 21 Apr.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: Activity was limited to a single planned task: refactoring scoped styles on Vue components (GDCU-8894), which was placed in the Backlog suggesting it is a future maintenance priority rather than an immediate focus. Jira updated 21 Apr. No Slack activity found for 21 Apr.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Jira shows two tickets updated on 21 Apr: GDCU-8856 (Iceland Launch Fixes) moved to Done, and GDCU-8810 (Set Up Organisation Level Developer Accounts for Apple/Google) remains In Progress. Both are active delivery cycle priorities. No Slack posts found for 21 Apr.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Resolved a display bug affecting site image carousels (GDCU-8830, Done) and moved refinements for the new Global Strategy Hub into the product phase (GDCU-8823, In Product). Currently focused on implementing event tags for Cosmo Spins and slot tournament games (GDCU-8824, In Progress) to enhance game tracking. All three Jira tickets confirmed active on 21 Apr.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Previously noted as being on annual leave in planning discussions (referenced by Gabriel Cornoiu in #gdc-pdmt on 13 Apr). No Jira assignments or Slack posts found.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: Focused on development of the new Image Carousel Shortcode (GDCU-8836) to enhance visual content delivery across the platform. The task has successfully progressed to the In Product stage, indicating the feature is moving toward final implementation. Jira confirmed updated 21 Apr. Josh Smith was coordinating with Nebojsa on image naming conventions for the shortcode.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Completed a hotfix for Cosmo Spins that updated the sign-in trigger to activate when users attempt to spin (GDCU-8548, Done — confirmed hotfix). Current priorities have shifted toward mobile and social integration: developing a bottom navigation bar (GDCU-8627, In Progress) and an Instagram Headers Widget for the New Zealand market (GDCU-8840, In Progress). All three tickets active in Jira on 21 Apr.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Completed several front-end updates including integrating preset table styles (GDCU-8604, Done), resolving image issues on the payment hub (GDCU-8832, Done), and removing prediction markets breadcrumb on US pages (GDCU-8844, Done). Moved AI football coverage (GDCU-8714) and Cloudflare bot management (GDCU-8792) into QA phase. Currently prioritising a fix for a race condition bug blocking desktop performance metrics due to geo-redirect issues (GDCU-8869, In Progress) — this is an active blocker. All tickets confirmed in Jira on 21 Apr.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Had limited activity, primarily focusing on a bug report regarding malfunctioning search results on the GDC Austria platform (GDCU-8827, logged in Backlog). No immediate progress was noted as the issue awaits further prioritisation. No Slack activity found for 21 Apr.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Shane was active in #gdc-pod on 21 Apr reporting three issues: (1) ACE AI feedback reports not generating since the weekend, (2) the IUJ section was missed in the site container width change causing layout issues, and (3) a recurring sign-in failure on article pages after sign-out. These were reported to Mike Pett and Carlos Blauth. All represent bugs surfaced through QA observation.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: Completed a brand data update for TalkSPORT BET involving removal of specific content under ticket ADS-9151. Activity was limited to this single task with no reported blockers or additional priorities. Last Slack activity was 15 Apr in #gdc-f2p-apps recommending the mobile app be restructured as a Cosmo App with a slots library focus.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Last substantive involvement was in #gdc-learning-hub in March, providing detailed content feedback and editing notes for the Learning Hub prior to launch.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: Focused on brand protection and negative SEO defence by conducting DMCA abuse audits across core sites and developing a group-level playbook. Prioritised automating the DMCA takedown process for the UK market via GetViktor and began building a centralised prompt library in Confluence to support these automation efforts. Supplementary: Active in #gdc-f2p-apps on 15 Apr flagging that some F2P games were not working and that Cosmo Spins had a 24-hour wait for free spins after login.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Focused on establishing organisation-level developer accounts for Apple and Google to streamline mobile platform management (GDCU-8810, In Progress — Harden is the assignee). Activity for 21 Apr was limited to this infrastructure priority. Supplementary: On 20 Apr in #gdc-pdmt he announced he is repurposing the PDMT meeting for planning every 3 weeks, with other weeks used as a weekly team sync.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Focused on coordinating brand reviews for various casino entities including Stake.com and Tonybet, while refining marketing compliance documentation and AI skill assets in Confluence. Actively managed identification of casino review pages via Slack to streamline the audit process and requested administrative support to adjust Slack channel visibility. Supplementary: On 16 Apr she announced the Iceland geo launch in #core-division, crediting the Product, Commercial, and Genesis teams for the cross-team effort.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Addressed a copyright-related notice from Google regarding the casinos.com domain and coordinated with team members to investigate the issue. Activity was otherwise limited with this specific compliance matter as primary focus. Supplementary: On 20 Apr in #core-division-leads he asked about experiences with Claude Design tool. Earlier posts (17 Apr) included announcing the Finance report as the single source of truth for revenue data, with the commercial report being discontinued.
